Please hear me out for your kindness' sake; that is our best request. Whoever does not ask for blessings like forgiveness, justifying righteousness, and eternal life must perish because of their lack. It is sad that so many make such a frightening choice. The psalmist warns against sin. Maintain a reverent respect for the glory and majesty of God. You have much to say to your hearts; speak with them, do not leave it unsaid. Examine them through serious self-reflection; let your thoughts focus on what is good, and stay close to it. Consider your ways, and before you go to sleep at night, examine your consciences regarding what you have done that day; especially anything you have done wrong, so that you may repent of it. When you wake up in the night, meditate on God and things related to your peace. On a sickbed, particularly, we should consider our ways. Be still. When you ask conscience a question, be serious, be silent, and wait for an answer. Do not open your mouth to excuse sin. All confidence must be in an answer; therefore, after commanding the sacrifices of righteousness, the psalmist says, Put your trust in the Lord.

People focused on worldly things seek good, but not the greatest good; they want outward good, present good, partial good, good food, good drink, a successful business, and a large estate. But what are all these worth? Any kind of good will satisfy most people, but a gracious soul will not be satisfied with that. Lord, let us have your favor, and let us know that we have it; we desire nothing more; let us be sure of your loving-kindness, and will be satisfied with it. Many seek happiness, but David had found it. When God puts grace in the heart, he puts gladness in the heart. Thus comforted, he felt compassion, but neither envied nor feared the most successful sinner. He commits all his affairs to God and is prepared to welcome his holy will. But salvation is in Christ alone; where will those appear who despise him as their Mediator and revile him with his disciples? May they stand in awe and no longer sin against the only remedy.
